1	"""
2	Slot sync manager — owns desired vs actual reconciliation.
3	
4	Compares entity states (active, PIN, name) against coordinator data (actual lock
5	code) and drives set/clear operations to reconcile. Uses a state machine
6	(SyncState enum) with periodic tick for retries and per-lock suspension on
7	repeated failures.
8	
9	Tick interval: 2 seconds (TICK_INTERVAL)
10	Circuit breaker: 3 attempts within 5 minutes (MAX_SYNC_ATTEMPTS, SYNC_ATTEMPT_WINDOW)
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	from collections.abc import Callable
16	from dataclasses import dataclass
17	from datetime import datetime
18	import logging
19	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
20	
21	from homeassistant.components.binary_sensor import DOMAIN as BINARY_SENSOR_DOMAIN
22	from homeassistant.components.sensor import DOMAIN as SENSOR_DOMAIN
23	from homeassistant.components.text import DOMAIN as TEXT_DOMAIN
24	from homeassistant.const import (
25	    CONF_NAME,
26	    CONF_PIN,
27	    STATE_OFF,
28	    STATE_ON,
29	    STATE_UNAVAILABLE,
30	    STATE_UNKNOWN,
31	)
32	from homeassistant.core import Event, EventStateChangedData, HomeAssistant, callback
33	from homeassistant.helpers import entity_registry as er
34	from homeassistant.helpers.event import (
35	    TrackStates,
36	    async_track_state_change_event,
37	    async_track_state_change_filtered,
38	    async_track_time_interval,
39	)
40	from homeassistant.helpers.issue_registry import (
41	    IssueSeverity,
42	    async_create_issue,
43	    async_delete_issue,
44	)
45	from homeassistant.util import dt as dt_util
46	
47	from .const import (
48	    ATTR_ACTIVE,
49	    ATTR_CODE,
50	    DOMAIN,
51	    MAX_SYNC_ATTEMPTS,
52	    SYNC_ATTEMPT_WINDOW,
53	    TICK_INTERVAL,
54	)
55	from .exceptions import CodeRejectedError, LockDisconnected, LockOperationFailed
56	from .models import SlotCode, SyncState
57	from .util import async_disable_slot
58	
59	if TYPE_CHECKING:
60	    from .coordinator import LockUsercodeUpdateCoordinator
61	    from .models import LockCodeManagerConfigEntry
62	    from .providers import BaseLock
63	
64	
65	_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)
66	
67	
68	@dataclass(frozen=True)
69	class SlotState:
70	    """
71	    Snapshot of entity states for a slot on a specific lock.
72	
73	    Used by SlotSyncManager to compare desired (entity) vs actual
74	    (coordinator) state. Includes raw HA state strings (rather than
75	    parsed values) because sync logic needs to distinguish between
76	    "off" and "unavailable" — both look like the same parsed bool but
77	    mean different things for retry decisions.
78	    """
79	
80	    active_state: str
81	    pin_state: str
82	    name_state: str | None
83	    code_state: str
84	    coordinator_code: str | SlotCode | None
85	
86	
87	class SlotSyncManager:
88	    """
89	    Manage sync state for a single lock x slot combination.
90	
91	    Compares desired state (from entity states: active, PIN) against actual
92	    state (from coordinator data) and drives set/clear operations to reconcile.
93	
94	    Uses a state machine (SyncState) with five states: LOADING, IN_SYNC,
95	    OUT_OF_SYNC, SYNCING, SUSPENDED. State changes mark the slot for
96	    re-evaluation; reconciliation happens on the next tick. Includes circuit
97	    breaker protection that suspends the lock after repeated sync failures.
98	
99	    The in-sync binary sensor entity reads manager.in_sync and
100	    manager.sync_status for display.
101	
102	    State mutation rules:
103	        - ``_request_sync_check`` transitions IN_SYNC -> OUT_OF_SYNC or
104	          SUSPENDED -> OUT_OF_SYNC for immediate UI feedback.
105	        - ``_async_tick_impl`` is the single authoritative place for all
106	          other state transitions, circuit breaker, sync operations,
107	          and ``_last_set_pin`` changes.
108	
109	    Lifecycle methods (async_start, async_stop) are idempotent and re-entrant.
110	    """
111	
112	    def __init__(
113	        self,
114	        hass: HomeAssistant,
115	        ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry,
116	        config_entry: LockCodeManagerConfigEntry,
117	        coordinator: LockUsercodeUpdateCoordinator,
118	        lock: BaseLock,
119	        slot_num: int,
120	        state_writer: Callable[[bool | None], None],
121	    ) -> None:
122	        """Initialize the sync manager."""
123	        self._hass = hass
124	        self._ent_reg = ent_reg
125	        self._config_entry = config_entry
126	        self._coordinator = coordinator
127	        self._lock = lock
128	        self._slot_num = int(slot_num)
129	        self._state_writer = state_writer
130	
131	        self._log_prefix = (
132	            f"{config_entry.entry_id} ({config_entry.title}): "
133	            f"{lock.lock.entity_id} slot {slot_num}"
134	        )
135	
136	        # Unique ID components for entity discovery
137	        entry_id = config_entry.entry_id
138	        lock_entity_id = lock.lock.entity_id
139	        base_uid = f"{entry_id}|{slot_num}"
140	        self._unique_ids: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {
141	            CONF_PIN: (TEXT_DOMAIN, f"{base_uid}|{CONF_PIN}"),
142	            CONF_NAME: (TEXT_DOMAIN, f"{base_uid}|{CONF_NAME}"),
143	            ATTR_ACTIVE: (BINARY_SENSOR_DOMAIN, f"{base_uid}|{ATTR_ACTIVE}"),
144	            ATTR_CODE: (SENSOR_DOMAIN, f"{base_uid}|{ATTR_CODE}|{lock_entity_id}"),
145	        }
146	
147	        # Sync state machine — single source of truth replacing _dirty,
148	        # _in_sync, and _tick_in_sync.
149	        self._state: SyncState = SyncState.LOADING
150	        self._entity_id_map: dict[str, str] = {}
151	        self._tracked_entity_ids: set[str] = set()
152	
153	        # Track the last PIN we successfully set, so we can detect when the
154	        # configured PIN changes while the lock code is UNKNOWN (masked/write-only).
155	        # This is in-memory only — on restart, _last_set_pin is None, which means
156	        # UNKNOWN slots will be treated as out-of-sync and re-set. This is the
157	        # safest behavior: it guarantees the lock has the correct PIN even if the
158	        # config changed while HA was down, at the cost of one extra set per
159	        # masked/write-only slot on every restart.
160	        self._last_set_pin: str | None = None
161	
162	        # Circuit breaker
163	        self._sync_attempt_count: int = 0
164	        self._sync_attempt_first: datetime | None = None
165	
166	        # Invalid state tracking (for initial load)
167	        self._logged_invalid_state: bool = False
168	
169	        # State tracking
170	        self._state_tracking_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = None
171	        self._coordinator_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = None
172	        self._tick_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = None
173	        self._tracking_all_states: bool = False
174	        self._started = False
175	
176	    @property
177	    def in_sync(self) -> bool | None:
178	        """Return current sync state (None = not yet determined)."""
179	        if self._state is SyncState.LOADING:
180	            return None
181	        return self._state is SyncState.IN_SYNC
182	
183	    @property
184	    def sync_status(self) -> str | None:
185	        """Return granular sync status for dashboard display."""
186	        if self._state is SyncState.LOADING:
187	            return None
188	        return self._state.value
189	
190	    async def async_start(self) -> None:
191	        """Start the sync manager -- discover entities, subscribe, initial tick."""
192	        if self._started:
193	            return
194	        self._started = True
195	        self._setup_state_tracking()
196	        self._setup_coordinator_listener()
197	        self._tick_unsub = async_track_time_interval(
198	            self._hass, self._async_tick, TICK_INTERVAL
199	        )
200	        await self._async_tick()
201	
202	    def async_stop(self) -> None:
203	        """Stop the sync manager -- unsubscribe tick and listeners. Idempotent."""
204	        if not self._started:
205	            return
206	        self._started = False
207	        if self._tick_unsub:
208	            self._tick_unsub()
209	            self._tick_unsub = None
210	        self._cleanup_state_tracking()
211	        if self._coordinator_unsub:
212	            self._coordinator_unsub()
213	            self._coordinator_unsub = None
214	        self._reset_sync_tracker()
215	
216	    # -- State resolution ----------------------------------------------------
217	
218	    def _get_entity_state(self, key: str) -> str | None:
219	        """Get entity state by role key."""
220	        entity_id = self._entity_id_map.get(key)
221	        if not entity_id:
222	            return None
223	        state = self._hass.states.get(entity_id)
224	        return state.state if state else None
225	
226	    def _build_entity_id_map(self) -> bool:
227	        """Build and cache entity IDs for this slot from the entity registry."""
228	        missing = False
229	        for key, (domain, unique_id) in self._unique_ids.items():
230	            if key in self._entity_id_map:
231	                continue
232	            ent_id = self._ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(domain, DOMAIN, unique_id)
233	            if not ent_id:
234	                missing = True
235	                continue
236	            self._entity_id_map[key] = ent_id
237	        self._tracked_entity_ids = set(self._entity_id_map.values())
238	        return not missing
239	
240	    def _ensure_entities_ready(self) -> bool:
241	        """
242	        Ensure all dependent entities exist with valid states.
243	
244	        The name entity (CONF_NAME) is optional — STATE_UNKNOWN is its
245	        normal state when no name is configured.
246	
247	        When the slot is inactive (active entity is OFF), PIN and code
248	        sensor entities are allowed to be STATE_UNKNOWN since the sync
249	        manager only needs to know the slot is off to proceed (clear or
250	        confirm already cleared). This prevents disabled slots from
251	        being stuck in LOADING forever.
252	        """
253	        # Collect all states in a single pass
254	        states: dict[str, str | None] = {}
255	        for key in (CONF_PIN, CONF_NAME, ATTR_ACTIVE, ATTR_CODE):
256	            if key not in self._entity_id_map:
257	                return False
258	            state = self._get_entity_state(key)
259	            if state is None or state == STATE_UNAVAILABLE:
260	                _LOGGER.debug("%s: Waiting for %s state", self._log_prefix, key)
261	                return False
262	            states[key] = state
263	
264	        # Active entity must always have a definite state
265	        if states[ATTR_ACTIVE] == STATE_UNKNOWN:
266	            _LOGGER.debug("%s: Waiting for %s state", self._log_prefix, ATTR_ACTIVE)
267	            return False
268	
269	        # Name is always optional (STATE_UNKNOWN = no name configured)
270	        # PIN and code sensor can be unknown when the slot is inactive
271	        slot_inactive = states[ATTR_ACTIVE] == STATE_OFF
272	        for key in (CONF_PIN, ATTR_CODE):
273	            if states[key] == STATE_UNKNOWN and not slot_inactive:
274	                _LOGGER.debug("%s: Waiting for %s state", self._log_prefix, key)
275	                return False
276	
277	        return True
278	
279	    def _resolve_slot_state(self) -> SlotState | None:
280	        """
281	        Resolve slot state from current entity and coordinator data.
282	
283	        All state reads are sync (hass.states.get) with no awaits between them,
284	        ensuring atomicity on Home Assistant's single-threaded event loop.
285	        """
286	        if not self._build_entity_id_map():
287	            return None
288	
289	        if self._slot_num not in self._coordinator.data:
290	            _LOGGER.debug(
291	                "%s: Slot not in coordinator data, skipping",
292	                self._log_prefix,
293	            )
294	            return None
295	
296	        if not self._ensure_entities_ready():
297	            return None
298	
299	        active_state = self._get_entity_state(ATTR_ACTIVE)
300	        pin_state = self._get_entity_state(CONF_PIN)
301	        name_state = self._get_entity_state(CONF_NAME)
302	        code_state = self._get_entity_state(ATTR_CODE)
303	
304	        if active_state is None or pin_state is None or code_state is None:
305	            return None
306	
307	        coordinator_code = self._coordinator.data.get(self._slot_num)
308	        return SlotState(
309	            active_state=active_state,
310	            pin_state=pin_state,
311	            name_state=name_state,
312	            code_state=code_state,
313	            coordinator_code=coordinator_code,
314	        )
315	
316	    # -- Sync calculation ----------------------------------------------------
317	
318	    def calculate_in_sync(self, slot_state: SlotState) -> bool:
319	        """
320	        Calculate whether slot should be in sync.
321	
322	        Active (state=ON): PIN should match code on lock.
323	        Inactive (state=OFF): Code on lock should be empty.
324	
325	        For UNKNOWN codes (masked/write-only): in sync only if the configured
326	        PIN matches what we last successfully set. This ensures that PIN
327	        changes trigger a re-set even when the lock code is unreadable, and
328	        that taking over a slot with an existing masked code triggers a set.
329	        """
330	        lock_code = (
331	            slot_state.coordinator_code
332	            if slot_state.coordinator_code is not None
333	            else slot_state.code_state
334	        )
335	        if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
336	            if lock_code is SlotCode.UNREADABLE_CODE:
337	                return slot_state.pin_state == self._last_set_pin
338	            if lock_code is SlotCode.EMPTY:
339	                # If we recently set a PIN on this slot and it matches the
340	                # configured PIN, trust the set — the provider may not have
341	                # caught up yet (eventual consistency, e.g. Schlage cloud API).
342	                return (
343	                    self._last_set_pin is not None
344	                    and slot_state.pin_state == self._last_set_pin
345	                )
346	            return slot_state.pin_state == lock_code
347	        # active_state == STATE_OFF: slot should be cleared
348	        # The "" check covers the fallback path where coordinator_code is None
349	        # and lock_code comes from the code sensor entity state (which returns ""
350	        # for SlotCode.EMPTY).
351	        return lock_code is SlotCode.EMPTY or lock_code == ""
352	
353	    # -- Sync execution ------------------------------------------------------
354	
355	    async def _perform_sync(self, slot_state: SlotState) -> None:
356	        """
357	        Execute sync operation (set or clear usercode).
358	
359	        Raises CodeRejectedError, LockDisconnected, or propagates any exception.
360	        Error handling is done by the caller (_async_tick).
361	        """
362	        if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
363	            await self._lock.async_internal_set_usercode(
364	                self._slot_num,
365	                slot_state.pin_state,
366	                slot_state.name_state,
367	                source="sync",
368	            )
369	            self._last_set_pin = slot_state.pin_state
370	            # Track set operations toward circuit breaker. Clear operations
371	            # don't increment the counter (expected to always succeed).
372	            self._record_sync_attempt()
373	            _LOGGER.debug("%s: Set usercode", self._log_prefix)
374	        else:
375	            await self._lock.async_internal_clear_usercode(
376	                self._slot_num, source="sync"
377	            )
378	            self._last_set_pin = None
379	            _LOGGER.debug("%s: Cleared usercode", self._log_prefix)
380	
381	    async def _disable_slot(self, reason: str) -> None:
382	        """Disable the slot and create a repair issue."""
383	        try:
384	            await async_disable_slot(
385	                self._hass,
386	                self._ent_reg,
387	                self._config_entry.entry_id,
388	                self._slot_num,
389	                reason=reason,
390	                lock_name=self._lock.display_name,
391	                lock_entity_id=self._lock.lock.entity_id,
392	            )
393	        except Exception:
394	            _LOGGER.exception(
395	                "%s: Failed to disable slot via service call",
396	                self._log_prefix,
397	            )
398	            # Fallback: create repair issue directly so the user is notified
399	            # even though the switch service call failed
400	            async_create_issue(
401	                self._hass,
402	                DOMAIN,
403	                f"slot_disabled_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._slot_num}",
404	                is_fixable=True,
405	                is_persistent=True,
406	                severity=IssueSeverity.WARNING,
407	                translation_key="slot_disabled",
408	                translation_placeholders={
409	                    "slot_num": str(self._slot_num),
410	                    "reason": reason,
411	                },
412	            )
413	        finally:
414	            self._reset_sync_tracker()
415	
416	    def _suspend_lock(self, reason: str) -> None:
417	        """Suspend this lock and create a per-lock repair issue."""
418	        self._state = SyncState.SUSPENDED
419	        self._coordinator.suspend_slot_sync_mgrs()
420	        self._reset_sync_tracker()
421	        self._write_state()
422	
423	        issue_id = (
424	            f"slot_suspended_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._lock.lock.entity_id}"
425	        )
426	        async_create_issue(
427	            self._hass,
428	            DOMAIN,
429	            issue_id,
430	            is_fixable=True,
431	            is_persistent=True,
432	            severity=IssueSeverity.WARNING,
433	            translation_key="slot_suspended",
434	            translation_placeholders={
435	                "lock_entity_id": self._lock.lock.entity_id,
436	                "lock_name": self._lock.display_name or self._lock.lock.entity_id,
437	                "reason": reason,
438	            },
439	        )
440	
441	    # -- Attempt tracking + circuit breaker ----------------------------------
442	
443	    def _reset_sync_tracker(self) -> None:
444	        """Reset the sync attempt tracker."""
445	        self._sync_attempt_count = 0
446	        self._sync_attempt_first = None
447	
448	    def _record_sync_attempt(self) -> None:
449	        """
450	        Record a sync attempt toward circuit breaker counter.
451	
452	        Called for set operations. Successful clear operations and transient
453	        LockDisconnected errors are not tracked since they represent
454	        transient lock communication issues, not persistent failures.
455	        """
456	        now = dt_util.utcnow()
457	        if (
458	            self._sync_attempt_first is not None
459	            and now - self._sync_attempt_first > SYNC_ATTEMPT_WINDOW
460	        ):
461	            self._sync_attempt_count = 0
462	            self._sync_attempt_first = None
463	
464	        if self._sync_attempt_first is None:
465	            self._sync_attempt_first = now
466	        self._sync_attempt_count += 1
467	
468	    def _sync_attempts_exceeded(self) -> bool:
469	        """Check if sync attempts exceeded the limit within the time window."""
470	        if self._sync_attempt_count < MAX_SYNC_ATTEMPTS:
471	            return False
472	        if self._sync_attempt_first is None:
473	            return False
474	        return dt_util.utcnow() - self._sync_attempt_first <= SYNC_ATTEMPT_WINDOW
475	
476	    # -- Orchestration -------------------------------------------------------
477	
478	    def _write_state(self) -> None:
479	        """Notify the entity to write Home Assistant state."""
480	        self._state_writer(self.in_sync)
481	
482	    @callback
483	    def _request_sync_check(self, *_args: Any) -> None:
484	        """
485	        Request a sync check on the next tick.
486	
487	        Transitions IN_SYNC -> OUT_OF_SYNC if calculate_in_sync returns False
488	        (also resets circuit breaker since the sync target changed).
489	        Transitions SUSPENDED -> OUT_OF_SYNC if the coordinator is no longer suspended.
490	        No-op for LOADING, OUT_OF_SYNC, SYNCING.
491	        """
492	        if self._state is SyncState.IN_SYNC:
493	            slot_state = self._resolve_slot_state()
494	            if slot_state is not None and not self.calculate_in_sync(slot_state):
495	                self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
496	                self._reset_sync_tracker()
497	                self._write_state()
498	        elif self._state is SyncState.SUSPENDED:
499	            if not self._coordinator.slot_sync_mgrs_suspended:
500	                self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
501	                self._write_state()
502	
503	    @callback
504	    def _request_sync_check_if_relevant(
505	        self, event: Event[EventStateChangedData]
506	    ) -> None:
507	        """
508	        Request sync check only if the state change is for a tracked entity.
509	
510	        Used by the catch-all state tracking fallback to avoid unnecessary
511	        checks on every HA state change. Falls back to always-checking if
512	        entity IDs haven't been discovered yet.
513	        """
514	        if not self._tracked_entity_ids or (
515	            event.data["entity_id"] in self._tracked_entity_ids
516	        ):
517	            self._request_sync_check()
518	
519	    async def _async_tick(self, _now: datetime | None = None) -> None:
520	        """Periodic reconciliation tick."""
521	        if not self._started:
522	            return
523	
524	        # Try upgrading before the state check — catch-all mode may prevent
525	        # _request_sync_check from firing for entities not yet tracked
526	        self._try_upgrade_state_tracking()
527	
528	        if self._state in (SyncState.IN_SYNC, SyncState.SYNCING, SyncState.SUSPENDED):
529	            return
530	
531	        await self._async_tick_impl()
532	
533	    async def _async_tick_impl(self) -> None:
534	        """
535	        Core tick logic — called from _async_tick for LOADING and OUT_OF_SYNC states.
536	
537	        This is the single authoritative place for all sync state mutations:
538	        circuit breaker tracking, sync operations, and ``_last_set_pin``
539	        changes.
540	        """
541	        slot_state = self._resolve_slot_state()
542	        if slot_state is None:
543	            # State resolution failed — stay in current state and retry.
544	            return
545	
546	        expected_in_sync = self.calculate_in_sync(slot_state)
547	
548	        # -- LOADING: detect initial sync state without performing operations --
549	        if self._state is SyncState.LOADING:
550	            if slot_state.active_state not in (STATE_ON, STATE_OFF):
551	                if not self._logged_invalid_state:
552	                    _LOGGER.debug(
553	                        "%s: Active entity has invalid state '%s', waiting "
554	                        "for valid state (ON/OFF)",
555	                        self._log_prefix,
556	                        slot_state.active_state,
557	                    )
558	                    self._logged_invalid_state = True
559	                return
560	
561	            if expected_in_sync:
562	                self._state = SyncState.IN_SYNC
563	                if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
564	                    async_delete_issue(
565	                        self._hass,
566	                        DOMAIN,
567	                        f"slot_disabled_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._slot_num}",
568	                    )
569	                # Per-lock issue: clear regardless of slot active state
570	                async_delete_issue(
571	                    self._hass,
572	                    DOMAIN,
573	                    f"slot_suspended_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._lock.lock.entity_id}",
574	                )
575	            else:
576	                self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
577	
578	            _LOGGER.debug(
579	                "%s: Initial state loaded, state=%s",
580	                self._log_prefix,
581	                self._state.value,
582	            )
583	            self._write_state()
584	            return
585	
586	        # -- OUT_OF_SYNC: check coordinator suspend flag, then attempt sync --
587	        if self._coordinator.slot_sync_mgrs_suspended:
588	            self._state = SyncState.SUSPENDED
589	            self._write_state()
590	            return
591	
592	        if expected_in_sync:
593	            # Became in sync without us doing anything (external change)
594	            self._state = SyncState.IN_SYNC
595	            self._reset_sync_tracker()
596	            self._write_state()
597	            if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
598	                async_delete_issue(
599	                    self._hass,
600	                    DOMAIN,
601	                    f"slot_disabled_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._slot_num}",
602	                )
603	            # Per-lock issue: clear regardless of slot active state
604	            async_delete_issue(
605	                self._hass,
606	                DOMAIN,
607	                f"slot_suspended_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._lock.lock.entity_id}",
608	            )
609	            return
610	
611	        # Circuit breaker check (set operations only)
612	        if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON and self._sync_attempts_exceeded():
613	            _LOGGER.error(
614	                "%s: Sync attempts exceeded (%s in %s window), suspending lock",
615	                self._log_prefix,
616	                self._sync_attempt_count,
617	                SYNC_ATTEMPT_WINDOW,
618	            )
619	            self._suspend_lock(
620	                f"Lock **{self._lock.lock.entity_id}**: slot "
621	                f"**{self._slot_num}** failed to sync after "
622	                f"{self._sync_attempt_count} consecutive attempts. "
623	                f"The lock may be rejecting the code silently or "
624	                f"experiencing communication issues. "
625	                f"Sync has been suspended for this lock. It will "
626	                f"resume automatically when the lock recovers.",
627	            )
628	            return
629	
630	        # Perform sync
631	        self._state = SyncState.SYNCING
632	        self._write_state()
633	        try:
634	            await self._perform_sync(slot_state)
635	        except CodeRejectedError as err:
636	            _LOGGER.error("%s: Code rejected: %s", self._log_prefix, err)
637	            await self._disable_slot(
638	                f"Lock **{err.lock_entity_id}**: slot **{err.code_slot}** "
639	                f"has been disabled. {err}\n\n"
640	                f"Fix the issue and re-enable the slot.",
641	            )
642	            # After disable, the slot active switch turns off. The next
643	            # _request_sync_check will see the slot as in-sync (no code
644	            # desired, no code on lock). Set to OUT_OF_SYNC so the next
645	            # tick resolves to IN_SYNC.
646	            self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
647	            return
648	        except (LockDisconnected, LockOperationFailed) as err:
649	            _LOGGER.info(
650	                "%s: Lock operation failed during %s usercode: %s. Will retry on next tick.",
651	                self._log_prefix,
652	                "set" if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON else "clear",
653	                err,
654	            )
655	            self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
656	            return
657	        except Exception as err:
658	            _LOGGER.exception(
659	                "%s: Unexpected error during %s usercode. "
660	                "Sync suspended for this lock to prevent infinite retry loop. "
661	                "Error: %s: %s",
662	                self._log_prefix,
663	                "set" if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON else "clear",
664	                type(err).__name__,
665	                err,
666	            )
667	            self._suspend_lock(
668	                f"Lock **{self._lock.lock.entity_id}**: slot **{self._slot_num}** "
669	                f"encountered an unexpected error during sync. This may indicate a bug "
670	                f"in the lock code manager integration. Check logs for details and "
671	                f"report this issue.\n\nError: {type(err).__name__}: {err}",
672	            )
673	            return
674	        else:
675	            # Sync succeeded — refresh coordinator to verify.
676	            # Skip for push providers — they update coordinator optimistically
677	            # via push_update() and refreshing from cache could read stale data.
678	            if not self._lock.supports_push:
679	                try:
680	                    await self._coordinator.async_refresh()
681	                except Exception:
682	                    _LOGGER.exception(
683	                        "%s: Coordinator refresh failed after sync operation. "
684	                        "Sync may have succeeded but verification failed. "
685	                        "Will retry on next tick.",
686	                        self._log_prefix,
687	                    )
688	                    self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
689	                    return
690	
691	        # Check if sync actually worked
692	        slot_state = self._resolve_slot_state()
693	        if slot_state is not None and self.calculate_in_sync(slot_state):
694	            self._state = SyncState.IN_SYNC
695	            self._reset_sync_tracker()
696	            self._write_state()
697	            if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
698	                async_delete_issue(
699	                    self._hass,
700	                    DOMAIN,
701	                    f"slot_disabled_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._slot_num}",
702	                )
703	            # Per-lock issue: clear regardless of slot active state
704	            async_delete_issue(
705	                self._hass,
706	                DOMAIN,
707	                f"slot_suspended_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._lock.lock.entity_id}",
708	            )
709	        else:
710	            self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
711	            self._write_state()
712	
713	    # -- State tracking subscriptions ----------------------------------------
714	
715	    def _setup_coordinator_listener(self) -> None:
716	        """Subscribe to coordinator updates."""
717	        self._coordinator_unsub = self._coordinator.async_add_listener(
718	            self._request_sync_check
719	        )
720	
721	    @callback
722	    def _cleanup_state_tracking(self) -> None:
723	        """Clean up state tracking subscription if one exists."""
724	        if self._state_tracking_unsub:
725	            self._state_tracking_unsub()
726	            self._state_tracking_unsub = None
727	            self._tracking_all_states = False
728	
729	    @callback
730	    def _try_upgrade_state_tracking(self) -> None:
731	        """
732	        Upgrade catch-all state tracking to targeted if entities are now available.
733	
734	        Called at the start of each tick (outside any callback), so it is safe
735	        to modify subscriptions here without timing issues.
736	        """
737	        if not self._tracking_all_states or not self._build_entity_id_map():
738	            return
739

742	        self._state_tracking_unsub = async_track_state_change_event(
743	            self._hass,
744	            self._tracked_entity_ids,

746	        )
747	        self._tracking_all_states = False
748	        _LOGGER.debug(
749	            "%s: Upgraded from catch-all to targeted state tracking",
750	            self._log_prefix,
751	        )

757

759	        Otherwise, tracks all state changes via a catch-all subscription that
760	        filters by tracked entity IDs in _request_sync_check_if_relevant. The catch-all
761	        is upgraded to targeted tracking in _try_upgrade_state_tracking(), which
762	        runs at the start of each tick (safe because it's outside a callback).

"""
Slot sync manager — owns desired vs actual reconciliation.

Compares entity states (active, PIN, name) against coordinator data (actual lock
code) and drives set/clear operations to reconcile. Uses a state machine
(SyncState enum) with periodic tick for retries and per-lock suspension on
repeated failures.

Tick interval: 2 seconds (TICK_INTERVAL)
Circuit breaker: 3 attempts within 5 minutes (MAX_SYNC_ATTEMPTS, SYNC_ATTEMPT_WINDOW)
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime
import logging
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any

from homeassistant.components.binary_sensor import DOMAIN as BINARY_SENSOR_DOMAIN
from homeassistant.components.sensor import DOMAIN as SENSOR_DOMAIN
from homeassistant.components.text import DOMAIN as TEXT_DOMAIN
from homeassistant.const import (
    CONF_NAME,
    CONF_PIN,
    STATE_OFF,
    STATE_ON,
    STATE_UNAVAILABLE,
    STATE_UNKNOWN,
)
from homeassistant.core import Event, EventStateChangedData, HomeAssistant, callback
from homeassistant.helpers import entity_registry as er
from homeassistant.helpers.event import (
    TrackStates,
    async_track_state_change_event,
    async_track_state_change_filtered,
    async_track_time_interval,
)
from homeassistant.helpers.issue_registry import (
    IssueSeverity,
    async_create_issue,
    async_delete_issue,
)
from homeassistant.util import dt as dt_util

from .const import (
    ATTR_ACTIVE,
    ATTR_CODE,
    DOMAIN,
    MAX_SYNC_ATTEMPTS,
    SYNC_ATTEMPT_WINDOW,
    TICK_INTERVAL,
)
from .exceptions import CodeRejectedError, LockDisconnected, LockOperationFailed
from .models import SlotCode, SyncState
from .util import async_disable_slot

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .coordinator import LockUsercodeUpdateCoordinator
    from .models import LockCodeManagerConfigEntry
    from .providers import BaseLock


_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SlotState:
    """
    Snapshot of entity states for a slot on a specific lock.

    Used by SlotSyncManager to compare desired (entity) vs actual
    (coordinator) state. Includes raw HA state strings (rather than
    parsed values) because sync logic needs to distinguish between
    "off" and "unavailable" — both look like the same parsed bool but
    mean different things for retry decisions.
    """

    active_state: str
    pin_state: str
    name_state: str | None
    code_state: str
    coordinator_code: str | SlotCode | None


class SlotSyncManager:
    """
    Manage sync state for a single lock x slot combination.

    Compares desired state (from entity states: active, PIN) against actual
    state (from coordinator data) and drives set/clear operations to reconcile.

    Uses a state machine (SyncState) with five states: LOADING, IN_SYNC,
    OUT_OF_SYNC, SYNCING, SUSPENDED. State changes mark the slot for
    re-evaluation; reconciliation happens on the next tick. Includes circuit
    breaker protection that suspends the lock after repeated sync failures.

    The in-sync binary sensor entity reads manager.in_sync and
    manager.sync_status for display.

    State mutation rules:
        - ``_request_sync_check`` transitions IN_SYNC -> OUT_OF_SYNC or
          SUSPENDED -> OUT_OF_SYNC for immediate UI feedback.
        - ``_async_tick_impl`` is the single authoritative place for all
          other state transitions, circuit breaker, sync operations,
          and ``_last_set_pin`` changes.

    Lifecycle methods (async_start, async_stop) are idempotent and re-entrant.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        hass: HomeAssistant,
        ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry,
        config_entry: LockCodeManagerConfigEntry,
        coordinator: LockUsercodeUpdateCoordinator,
        lock: BaseLock,
        slot_num: int,
        state_writer: Callable[[bool | None], None],
    ) -> None:
        """Initialize the sync manager."""
        self._hass = hass
        self._ent_reg = ent_reg
        self._config_entry = config_entry
        self._coordinator = coordinator
        self._lock = lock
        self._slot_num = int(slot_num)
        self._state_writer = state_writer

        self._log_prefix = (
            f"{config_entry.entry_id} ({config_entry.title}): "
            f"{lock.lock.entity_id} slot {slot_num}"
        )

        # Unique ID components for entity discovery
        entry_id = config_entry.entry_id
        lock_entity_id = lock.lock.entity_id
        base_uid = f"{entry_id}|{slot_num}"
        self._unique_ids: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {
            CONF_PIN: (TEXT_DOMAIN, f"{base_uid}|{CONF_PIN}"),
            CONF_NAME: (TEXT_DOMAIN, f"{base_uid}|{CONF_NAME}"),
            ATTR_ACTIVE: (BINARY_SENSOR_DOMAIN, f"{base_uid}|{ATTR_ACTIVE}"),
            ATTR_CODE: (SENSOR_DOMAIN, f"{base_uid}|{ATTR_CODE}|{lock_entity_id}"),
        }

        # Sync state machine — single source of truth replacing _dirty,
        # _in_sync, and _tick_in_sync.
        self._state: SyncState = SyncState.LOADING
        self._entity_id_map: dict[str, str] = {}
        self._tracked_entity_ids: set[str] = set()

        # Track the last PIN we successfully set, so we can detect when the
        # configured PIN changes while the lock code is UNKNOWN (masked/write-only).
        # This is in-memory only — on restart, _last_set_pin is None, which means
        # UNKNOWN slots will be treated as out-of-sync and re-set. This is the
        # safest behavior: it guarantees the lock has the correct PIN even if the
        # config changed while HA was down, at the cost of one extra set per
        # masked/write-only slot on every restart.
        self._last_set_pin: str | None = None

        # Circuit breaker
        self._sync_attempt_count: int = 0
        self._sync_attempt_first: datetime | None = None

        # Invalid state tracking (for initial load)
        self._logged_invalid_state: bool = False

        # State tracking
        self._state_tracking_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = None
        self._coordinator_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = None
        self._tick_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = None
        self._tracking_all_states: bool = False
        self._started = False

    @property
    def in_sync(self) -> bool | None:
        """Return current sync state (None = not yet determined)."""
        if self._state is SyncState.LOADING:
            return None
        return self._state is SyncState.IN_SYNC

    @property
    def sync_status(self) -> str | None:
        """Return granular sync status for dashboard display."""
        if self._state is SyncState.LOADING:
            return None
        return self._state.value

    async def async_start(self) -> None:
        """Start the sync manager -- discover entities, subscribe, initial tick."""
        if self._started:
            return
        self._started = True
        self._setup_state_tracking()
        self._setup_coordinator_listener()
        self._tick_unsub = async_track_time_interval(
            self._hass, self._async_tick, TICK_INTERVAL
        )
        await self._async_tick()

    def async_stop(self) -> None:
        """Stop the sync manager -- unsubscribe tick and listeners. Idempotent."""
        if not self._started:
            return
        self._started = False
        if self._tick_unsub:
            self._tick_unsub()
            self._tick_unsub = None
        self._cleanup_state_tracking()
        if self._coordinator_unsub:
            self._coordinator_unsub()
            self._coordinator_unsub = None
        self._reset_sync_tracker()

    # -- State resolution ----------------------------------------------------

    def _get_entity_state(self, key: str) -> str | None:
        """Get entity state by role key."""
        entity_id = self._entity_id_map.get(key)
        if not entity_id:
            return None
        state = self._hass.states.get(entity_id)
        return state.state if state else None

    def _build_entity_id_map(self) -> bool:
        """Build and cache entity IDs for this slot from the entity registry."""
        missing = False
        for key, (domain, unique_id) in self._unique_ids.items():
            if key in self._entity_id_map:
                continue
            ent_id = self._ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(domain, DOMAIN, unique_id)
            if not ent_id:
                missing = True
                continue
            self._entity_id_map[key] = ent_id
        self._tracked_entity_ids = set(self._entity_id_map.values())
        return not missing

    def _ensure_entities_ready(self) -> bool:
        """
        Ensure all dependent entities exist with valid states.

        The name entity (CONF_NAME) is optional — STATE_UNKNOWN is its
        normal state when no name is configured.

        When the slot is inactive (active entity is OFF), PIN and code
        sensor entities are allowed to be STATE_UNKNOWN since the sync
        manager only needs to know the slot is off to proceed (clear or
        confirm already cleared). This prevents disabled slots from
        being stuck in LOADING forever.
        """
        # Collect all states in a single pass
        states: dict[str, str | None] = {}
        for key in (CONF_PIN, CONF_NAME, ATTR_ACTIVE, ATTR_CODE):
            if key not in self._entity_id_map:
                return False
            state = self._get_entity_state(key)
            if state is None or state == STATE_UNAVAILABLE:
                _LOGGER.debug("%s: Waiting for %s state", self._log_prefix, key)
                return False
            states[key] = state

        # Active entity must always have a definite state
        if states[ATTR_ACTIVE] == STATE_UNKNOWN:
            _LOGGER.debug("%s: Waiting for %s state", self._log_prefix, ATTR_ACTIVE)
            return False

        # Name is always optional (STATE_UNKNOWN = no name configured)
        # PIN and code sensor can be unknown when the slot is inactive
        slot_inactive = states[ATTR_ACTIVE] == STATE_OFF
        for key in (CONF_PIN, ATTR_CODE):
            if states[key] == STATE_UNKNOWN and not slot_inactive:
                _LOGGER.debug("%s: Waiting for %s state", self._log_prefix, key)
                return False

        return True

    def _resolve_slot_state(self) -> SlotState | None:
        """
        Resolve slot state from current entity and coordinator data.

        All state reads are sync (hass.states.get) with no awaits between them,
        ensuring atomicity on Home Assistant's single-threaded event loop.
        """
        if not self._build_entity_id_map():
            return None

        if self._slot_num not in self._coordinator.data:
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "%s: Slot not in coordinator data, skipping",
                self._log_prefix,
            )
            return None

        if not self._ensure_entities_ready():
            return None

        active_state = self._get_entity_state(ATTR_ACTIVE)
        pin_state = self._get_entity_state(CONF_PIN)
        name_state = self._get_entity_state(CONF_NAME)
        code_state = self._get_entity_state(ATTR_CODE)

        if active_state is None or pin_state is None or code_state is None:
            return None

        coordinator_code = self._coordinator.data.get(self._slot_num)
        return SlotState(
            active_state=active_state,
            pin_state=pin_state,
            name_state=name_state,
            code_state=code_state,
            coordinator_code=coordinator_code,
        )

    # -- Sync calculation ----------------------------------------------------

    def calculate_in_sync(self, slot_state: SlotState) -> bool:
        """
        Calculate whether slot should be in sync.

        Active (state=ON): PIN should match code on lock.
        Inactive (state=OFF): Code on lock should be empty.

        For UNKNOWN codes (masked/write-only): in sync only if the configured
        PIN matches what we last successfully set. This ensures that PIN
        changes trigger a re-set even when the lock code is unreadable, and
        that taking over a slot with an existing masked code triggers a set.
        """
        lock_code = (
            slot_state.coordinator_code
            if slot_state.coordinator_code is not None
            else slot_state.code_state
        )
        if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
            if lock_code is SlotCode.UNREADABLE_CODE:
                return slot_state.pin_state == self._last_set_pin
            if lock_code is SlotCode.EMPTY:
                # If we recently set a PIN on this slot and it matches the
                # configured PIN, trust the set — the provider may not have
                # caught up yet (eventual consistency, e.g. Schlage cloud API).
                return (
                    self._last_set_pin is not None
                    and slot_state.pin_state == self._last_set_pin
                )
            return slot_state.pin_state == lock_code
        # active_state == STATE_OFF: slot should be cleared
        # The "" check covers the fallback path where coordinator_code is None
        # and lock_code comes from the code sensor entity state (which returns ""
        # for SlotCode.EMPTY).
        return lock_code is SlotCode.EMPTY or lock_code == ""

    # -- Sync execution ------------------------------------------------------

    async def _perform_sync(self, slot_state: SlotState) -> None:
        """
        Execute sync operation (set or clear usercode).

        Raises CodeRejectedError, LockDisconnected, or propagates any exception.
        Error handling is done by the caller (_async_tick).
        """
        if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
            await self._lock.async_internal_set_usercode(
                self._slot_num,
                slot_state.pin_state,
                slot_state.name_state,
                source="sync",
            )
            self._last_set_pin = slot_state.pin_state
            # Track set operations toward circuit breaker. Clear operations
            # don't increment the counter (expected to always succeed).
            self._record_sync_attempt()
            _LOGGER.debug("%s: Set usercode", self._log_prefix)
        else:
            await self._lock.async_internal_clear_usercode(
                self._slot_num, source="sync"
            )
            self._last_set_pin = None
            _LOGGER.debug("%s: Cleared usercode", self._log_prefix)

    async def _disable_slot(self, reason: str) -> None:
        """Disable the slot and create a repair issue."""
        try:
            await async_disable_slot(
                self._hass,
                self._ent_reg,
                self._config_entry.entry_id,
                self._slot_num,
                reason=reason,
                lock_name=self._lock.display_name,
                lock_entity_id=self._lock.lock.entity_id,
            )
        except Exception:
            _LOGGER.exception(
                "%s: Failed to disable slot via service call",
                self._log_prefix,
            )
            # Fallback: create repair issue directly so the user is notified
            # even though the switch service call failed
            async_create_issue(
                self._hass,
                DOMAIN,
                f"slot_disabled_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._slot_num}",
                is_fixable=True,
                is_persistent=True,
                severity=IssueSeverity.WARNING,
                translation_key="slot_disabled",
                translation_placeholders={
                    "slot_num": str(self._slot_num),
                    "reason": reason,
                },
            )
        finally:
            self._reset_sync_tracker()

    def _suspend_lock(self, reason: str) -> None:
        """Suspend this lock and create a per-lock repair issue."""
        self._state = SyncState.SUSPENDED
        self._coordinator.suspend_slot_sync_mgrs()
        self._reset_sync_tracker()
        self._write_state()

        issue_id = (
            f"slot_suspended_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._lock.lock.entity_id}"
        )
        async_create_issue(
            self._hass,
            DOMAIN,
            issue_id,
            is_fixable=True,
            is_persistent=True,
            severity=IssueSeverity.WARNING,
            translation_key="slot_suspended",
            translation_placeholders={
                "lock_entity_id": self._lock.lock.entity_id,
                "lock_name": self._lock.display_name or self._lock.lock.entity_id,
                "reason": reason,
            },
        )

    # -- Attempt tracking + circuit breaker ----------------------------------

    def _reset_sync_tracker(self) -> None:
        """Reset the sync attempt tracker."""
        self._sync_attempt_count = 0
        self._sync_attempt_first = None

    def _record_sync_attempt(self) -> None:
        """
        Record a sync attempt toward circuit breaker counter.

        Called for set operations. Successful clear operations and transient
        LockDisconnected errors are not tracked since they represent
        transient lock communication issues, not persistent failures.
        """
        now = dt_util.utcnow()
        if (
            self._sync_attempt_first is not None
            and now - self._sync_attempt_first > SYNC_ATTEMPT_WINDOW
        ):
            self._sync_attempt_count = 0
            self._sync_attempt_first = None

        if self._sync_attempt_first is None:
            self._sync_attempt_first = now
        self._sync_attempt_count += 1

    def _sync_attempts_exceeded(self) -> bool:
        """Check if sync attempts exceeded the limit within the time window."""
        if self._sync_attempt_count < MAX_SYNC_ATTEMPTS:
            return False
        if self._sync_attempt_first is None:
            return False
        return dt_util.utcnow() - self._sync_attempt_first <= SYNC_ATTEMPT_WINDOW

    # -- Orchestration -------------------------------------------------------

    def _write_state(self) -> None:
        """Notify the entity to write Home Assistant state."""
        self._state_writer(self.in_sync)

    @callback
    def _request_sync_check(self, *_args: Any) -> None:
        """
        Request a sync check on the next tick.

        Transitions IN_SYNC -> OUT_OF_SYNC if calculate_in_sync returns False
        (also resets circuit breaker since the sync target changed).
        Transitions SUSPENDED -> OUT_OF_SYNC if the coordinator is no longer suspended.
        No-op for LOADING, OUT_OF_SYNC, SYNCING.
        """
        if self._state is SyncState.IN_SYNC:
            slot_state = self._resolve_slot_state()
            if slot_state is not None and not self.calculate_in_sync(slot_state):
                self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
                self._reset_sync_tracker()
                self._write_state()
        elif self._state is SyncState.SUSPENDED:
            if not self._coordinator.slot_sync_mgrs_suspended:
                self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
                self._write_state()

    @callback
    def _request_sync_check_if_relevant(
        self, event: Event[EventStateChangedData]
    ) -> None:
        """
        Request sync check only if the state change is for a tracked entity.

        Used by the catch-all state tracking fallback to avoid unnecessary
        checks on every HA state change. Falls back to always-checking if
        entity IDs haven't been discovered yet.
        """
        if not self._tracked_entity_ids or (
            event.data["entity_id"] in self._tracked_entity_ids
        ):
            self._request_sync_check()

    async def _async_tick(self, _now: datetime | None = None) -> None:
        """Periodic reconciliation tick."""
        if not self._started:
            return

        # Try upgrading before the state check — catch-all mode may prevent
        # _request_sync_check from firing for entities not yet tracked
        self._try_upgrade_state_tracking()

        if self._state in (SyncState.IN_SYNC, SyncState.SYNCING, SyncState.SUSPENDED):
            return

        await self._async_tick_impl()

    async def _async_tick_impl(self) -> None:
        """
        Core tick logic — called from _async_tick for LOADING and OUT_OF_SYNC states.

        This is the single authoritative place for all sync state mutations:
        circuit breaker tracking, sync operations, and ``_last_set_pin``
        changes.
        """
        slot_state = self._resolve_slot_state()
        if slot_state is None:
            # State resolution failed — stay in current state and retry.
            return

        expected_in_sync = self.calculate_in_sync(slot_state)

        # -- LOADING: detect initial sync state without performing operations --
        if self._state is SyncState.LOADING:
            if slot_state.active_state not in (STATE_ON, STATE_OFF):
                if not self._logged_invalid_state:
                    _LOGGER.debug(
                        "%s: Active entity has invalid state '%s', waiting "
                        "for valid state (ON/OFF)",
                        self._log_prefix,
                        slot_state.active_state,
                    )
                    self._logged_invalid_state = True
                return

            if expected_in_sync:
                self._state = SyncState.IN_SYNC
                if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
                    async_delete_issue(
                        self._hass,
                        DOMAIN,
                        f"slot_disabled_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._slot_num}",
                    )
                # Per-lock issue: clear regardless of slot active state
                async_delete_issue(
                    self._hass,
                    DOMAIN,
                    f"slot_suspended_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._lock.lock.entity_id}",
                )
            else:
                self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC

            _LOGGER.debug(
                "%s: Initial state loaded, state=%s",
                self._log_prefix,
                self._state.value,
            )
            self._write_state()
            return

        # -- OUT_OF_SYNC: check coordinator suspend flag, then attempt sync --
        if self._coordinator.slot_sync_mgrs_suspended:
            self._state = SyncState.SUSPENDED
            self._write_state()
            return

        if expected_in_sync:
            # Became in sync without us doing anything (external change)
            self._state = SyncState.IN_SYNC
            self._reset_sync_tracker()
            self._write_state()
            if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
                async_delete_issue(
                    self._hass,
                    DOMAIN,
                    f"slot_disabled_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._slot_num}",
                )
            # Per-lock issue: clear regardless of slot active state
            async_delete_issue(
                self._hass,
                DOMAIN,
                f"slot_suspended_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._lock.lock.entity_id}",
            )
            return

        # Circuit breaker check (set operations only)
        if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON and self._sync_attempts_exceeded():
            _LOGGER.error(
                "%s: Sync attempts exceeded (%s in %s window), suspending lock",
                self._log_prefix,
                self._sync_attempt_count,
                SYNC_ATTEMPT_WINDOW,
            )
            self._suspend_lock(
                f"Lock **{self._lock.lock.entity_id}**: slot "
                f"**{self._slot_num}** failed to sync after "
                f"{self._sync_attempt_count} consecutive attempts. "
                f"The lock may be rejecting the code silently or "
                f"experiencing communication issues. "
                f"Sync has been suspended for this lock. It will "
                f"resume automatically when the lock recovers.",
            )
            return

        # Perform sync
        self._state = SyncState.SYNCING
        self._write_state()
        try:
            await self._perform_sync(slot_state)
        except CodeRejectedError as err:
            _LOGGER.error("%s: Code rejected: %s", self._log_prefix, err)
            await self._disable_slot(
                f"Lock **{err.lock_entity_id}**: slot **{err.code_slot}** "
                f"has been disabled. {err}\n\n"
                f"Fix the issue and re-enable the slot.",
            )
            # After disable, the slot active switch turns off. The next
            # _request_sync_check will see the slot as in-sync (no code
            # desired, no code on lock). Set to OUT_OF_SYNC so the next
            # tick resolves to IN_SYNC.
            self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
            return
        except (LockDisconnected, LockOperationFailed) as err:
            _LOGGER.info(
                "%s: Lock operation failed during %s usercode: %s. Will retry on next tick.",
                self._log_prefix,
                "set" if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON else "clear",
                err,
            )
            self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
            return
        except Exception as err:
            _LOGGER.exception(
                "%s: Unexpected error during %s usercode. "
                "Sync suspended for this lock to prevent infinite retry loop. "
                "Error: %s: %s",
                self._log_prefix,
                "set" if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON else "clear",
                type(err).__name__,
                err,
            )
            self._suspend_lock(
                f"Lock **{self._lock.lock.entity_id}**: slot **{self._slot_num}** "
                f"encountered an unexpected error during sync. This may indicate a bug "
                f"in the lock code manager integration. Check logs for details and "
                f"report this issue.\n\nError: {type(err).__name__}: {err}",
            )
            return
        else:
            # Sync succeeded — refresh coordinator to verify.
            # Skip for push providers — they update coordinator optimistically
            # via push_update() and refreshing from cache could read stale data.
            if not self._lock.supports_push:
                try:
                    await self._coordinator.async_refresh()
                except Exception:
                    _LOGGER.exception(
                        "%s: Coordinator refresh failed after sync operation. "
                        "Sync may have succeeded but verification failed. "
                        "Will retry on next tick.",
                        self._log_prefix,
                    )
                    self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
                    return

        # Check if sync actually worked
        slot_state = self._resolve_slot_state()
        if slot_state is not None and self.calculate_in_sync(slot_state):
            self._state = SyncState.IN_SYNC
            self._reset_sync_tracker()
            self._write_state()
            if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
                async_delete_issue(
                    self._hass,
                    DOMAIN,
                    f"slot_disabled_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._slot_num}",
                )
            # Per-lock issue: clear regardless of slot active state
            async_delete_issue(
                self._hass,
                DOMAIN,
                f"slot_suspended_{self._config_entry.entry_id}_{self._lock.lock.entity_id}",
            )
        else:
            self._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
            self._write_state()

    # -- State tracking subscriptions ----------------------------------------

    def _setup_coordinator_listener(self) -> None:
        """Subscribe to coordinator updates."""
        self._coordinator_unsub = self._coordinator.async_add_listener(
            self._request_sync_check
        )

    @callback
    def _cleanup_state_tracking(self) -> None:
        """Clean up state tracking subscription if one exists."""
        if self._state_tracking_unsub:
            self._state_tracking_unsub()
            self._state_tracking_unsub = None
            self._tracking_all_states = False

    @callback
    def _try_upgrade_state_tracking(self) -> None:
        """
        Upgrade catch-all state tracking to targeted if entities are now available.

        Called at the start of each tick (outside any callback), so it is safe
        to modify subscriptions here without timing issues.
        """
        if not self._tracking_all_states or not self._build_entity_id_map():
            return

        assert self._state_tracking_unsub is not None
        self._state_tracking_unsub()
        self._state_tracking_unsub = async_track_state_change_event(
            self._hass,
            self._tracked_entity_ids,
            self._request_sync_check,
        )
        self._tracking_all_states = False
        _LOGGER.debug(
            "%s: Upgraded from catch-all to targeted state tracking",
            self._log_prefix,
        )

    @callback
    def _setup_state_tracking(self) -> None:
        """
        Set up state change tracking for dependent entities.

        If all entity IDs are available, tracks only those specific entities.
        Otherwise, tracks all state changes via a catch-all subscription that
        filters by tracked entity IDs in _request_sync_check_if_relevant. The catch-all
        is upgraded to targeted tracking in _try_upgrade_state_tracking(), which
        runs at the start of each tick (safe because it's outside a callback).
        """
        self._cleanup_state_tracking()

        if self._build_entity_id_map():
            self._state_tracking_unsub = async_track_state_change_event(
                self._hass,
                self._tracked_entity_ids,
                self._request_sync_check,
            )
            self._tracking_all_states = False
        else:
            tracker = async_track_state_change_filtered(
                self._hass,
                TrackStates(True, set(), set()),
                self._request_sync_check_if_relevant,
            )
            self._state_tracking_unsub = tracker.async_remove
            self._tracking_all_states = True
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "%s: Waiting for dependent entities, tracking all state changes",
                self._log_prefix,
            )

127	        self._consecutive_failures += 1

132	                BACKOFF_MAX_SECONDS,

173	        """
174	        Suspend slot sync managers for this lock.
175

180	        """

184	        # transition to SUSPENDED on their next _request_sync_check.

188	        """Reset failure counter and restore original update interval."""
189	        self._slot_sync_mgrs_suspended = False
190	        if self._consecutive_failures > 0:
191	            _LOGGER.info(
192	                "Lock %s recovered after %d consecutive failures",
193	                self._lock.lock.entity_id,
194	                self._consecutive_failures,
195	            )
196	            self._consecutive_failures = 0
197	            if self._original_update_interval is not None:
198	                self.update_interval = self._original_update_interval
199	        # Unconditionally clear lock_offline issue on any successful poll.
200	        # Runs outside the if-block so it also clears persisted issues that
201	        # survive HA restarts (where _consecutive_failures resets to 0).
202	        async_delete_issue(
203	            self.hass,
204	            DOMAIN,
205	            f"lock_offline_{self._lock.lock.entity_id}",
206	        )
207	
208	    async def async_get_usercodes(self) -> dict[int, str | SlotCode]:
209	        """Fetch usercodes from the provider, normalize slot keys to int, and apply backoff handling."""
210	        try:
211	            data = await self._lock.async_internal_get_usercodes()
212	        except LockCodeManagerError as err:
213	            self._apply_backoff()
214	            # During cold start (before the first successful poll), do not
215	            # raise UpdateFailed. That would fail the initial refresh and
216	            # keep coordinator-backed entities unavailable until a
217	            # successful poll completes.
218	            if not self.last_update_success:
219	                return {}
220	            raise UpdateFailed from err
221	
222	        self._reset_backoff()
223	        return self._normalize_keys(data)
224	
225	    async def _async_drift_check(self, now: datetime) -> None:
226	        """
227	        Perform periodic drift detection.
228	
229	        Hard refresh re-fetches codes from the lock to detect out-of-band changes
230	        (e.g., codes changed at the lock's keypad). If changes are detected,
231	        updates coordinator data and notifies listeners.
232	        """
233	        # Skip if we haven't successfully loaded initial data yet
234	        if not self.last_update_success:
235	            return
236	
237	        if self._consecutive_failures >= BACKOFF_FAILURE_THRESHOLD:
238	            _LOGGER.debug(
239	                "Skipping drift check for %s (in backoff after %d failures)",
240	                self._lock.lock.entity_id,
241	                self._consecutive_failures,
242	            )
243	            return
244	
245	        _LOGGER.debug(
246	            "Performing drift detection hard refresh for %s",
247	            self._lock.lock.entity_id,
248	        )
249	        try:
250	            new_data = self._normalize_keys(
251	                await self._lock.async_internal_hard_refresh_codes()
252	            )
253	        except LockCodeManagerError as err:
254	            self._apply_backoff()
255	            _LOGGER.warning(
256	                "Drift detection hard refresh failed for %s: %s",
257	                self._lock.lock.entity_id,
258	                err,
259	            )
260	            return
261	
262	        # Push subscription retry is handled by the config entry state
263	        # listener and connection transition handler — no need to retry here.
264	
265	        if new_data != self.data:
266	            _LOGGER.debug(
267	                "Drift detected for %s, updating coordinator data",
268	                self._lock.lock.entity_id,
269	            )
270	            self.async_set_updated_data(new_data)
271	
272	    async def _async_connection_check(self, now: datetime) -> None:
273	        """Poll connection state so providers can resubscribe on reconnect."""
274	        try:
275	            await self._lock.async_internal_is_integration_connected()
276	        except LockCodeManagerError as err:
277	            _LOGGER.debug(
278	                "Connection check failed for %s: %s", self._lock.lock.entity_id, err
279	            )
280	
281	    async def async_shutdown(self) -> None:
282	        """Shut down the coordinator and clean up resources."""
283	        if self._drift_unsub:
284	            self._drift_unsub()
285	            self._drift_unsub = None
286	        if self._connection_unsub:
287	            self._connection_unsub()
288	            self._connection_unsub = None
289	        await super().async_shutdown()

"""
Manages the slot->code mapping for a single lock.

Stores ALL slots (managed and unmanaged). See ARCHITECTURE.md for the full data flow.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Callable
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import logging
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any

from homeassistant.const import CONF_ENABLED, CONF_PIN
from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant, callback
from homeassistant.helpers.event import async_track_time_interval
from homeassistant.helpers.issue_registry import (
    IssueSeverity,
    async_create_issue,
    async_delete_issue,
)
from homeassistant.helpers.update_coordinator import DataUpdateCoordinator, UpdateFailed

from .const import (
    BACKOFF_FAILURE_THRESHOLD,
    BACKOFF_INITIAL_SECONDS,
    BACKOFF_MAX_SECONDS,
    DOMAIN,
    POLL_FAILURE_ALERT_THRESHOLD,
)
from .data import get_entry_config
from .exceptions import LockCodeManagerError
from .models import SlotCode

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .providers import BaseLock

_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class LockUsercodeUpdateCoordinator(DataUpdateCoordinator[dict[int, str | SlotCode]]):
    """Class to manage usercode updates."""

    def __init__(self, hass: HomeAssistant, lock: BaseLock, config_entry: Any) -> None:
        """Initialize the usercode update coordinator."""
        self._lock = lock
        self._drift_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = None
        self._connection_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = None
        # Disable periodic polling when push updates are supported.
        # Polling is still used for initial load.
        update_interval = None if lock.supports_push else lock.usercode_scan_interval
        super().__init__(
            hass,
            _LOGGER,
            name=f"{DOMAIN} {lock.lock.entity_id}",
            update_method=self.async_get_usercodes,
            update_interval=update_interval,
            config_entry=config_entry,
        )
        self.data: dict[int, str | SlotCode] = {}
        self._slot_sync_mgrs_suspended: bool = False
        self._config_entry = config_entry
        self._consecutive_failures: int = 0
        self._original_update_interval: timedelta | None = update_interval

        # Set up drift detection timer for locks with hard_refresh_interval
        if lock.hard_refresh_interval:
            self._drift_unsub = async_track_time_interval(
                hass,
                self._async_drift_check,
                lock.hard_refresh_interval,
                cancel_on_shutdown=True,
            )

        if lock.connection_check_interval:
            # Periodic connection checks drive reconnect handling for non-push providers.
            self._connection_unsub = async_track_time_interval(
                hass,
                self._async_connection_check,
                lock.connection_check_interval,
                cancel_on_shutdown=True,
            )

    @property
    def lock(self) -> BaseLock:
        """Return the lock."""
        return self._lock

    def get_expected_pin(self, slot_num: int) -> str | None:
        """Return configured PIN for a slot, or None if disabled/unconfigured."""
        slot_data = get_entry_config(self._config_entry).slot(slot_num)
        if not slot_data.get(CONF_ENABLED):
            return None
        return slot_data.get(CONF_PIN) or None

    def slot_expects_pin(self, slot_num: int) -> bool:
        """Return whether LCM expects a PIN on this slot (enabled with PIN)."""
        return self.get_expected_pin(slot_num) is not None

    @staticmethod
    def _normalize_keys(
        data: dict[Any, str | SlotCode],
    ) -> dict[int, str | SlotCode]:
        """Coerce slot keys to ``int``. Raises ValueError/TypeError if a key cannot be cast."""
        return {int(k): v for k, v in data.items()}

    @callback
    def push_update(self, updates: dict[int, str | SlotCode]) -> None:
        """Push one or more slot updates and notify listening entities."""
        if not updates:
            return

        new_data = {**self.data, **self._normalize_keys(updates)}
        # Skip update if data hasn't actually changed to avoid redundant logging
        # and unnecessary listener notifications
        if new_data == self.data:
            return

        # A successful push update proves the lock is reachable, so reset
        # backoff to re-enable drift checks and normal polling.
        self._reset_backoff()

        self.async_set_updated_data(new_data)

    def _apply_backoff(self) -> None:
        """Increment failure counter and apply exponential backoff if threshold met."""
        self._consecutive_failures += 1
        if self._consecutive_failures >= BACKOFF_FAILURE_THRESHOLD:
            backoff_secs = min(
                BACKOFF_INITIAL_SECONDS
                * 2 ** (self._consecutive_failures - BACKOFF_FAILURE_THRESHOLD),
                BACKOFF_MAX_SECONDS,
            )
            if self._original_update_interval is not None:
                new_interval = timedelta(seconds=backoff_secs)
                if new_interval != self.update_interval:  # type: ignore[has-type]
                    self.update_interval = new_interval
                    _LOGGER.warning(
                        "Update failed %d consecutive times for %s, "
                        "backing off polling interval to %ds",
                        self._consecutive_failures,
                        self._lock.lock.entity_id,
                        backoff_secs,
                    )
            else:
                _LOGGER.warning(
                    "Update failed %d consecutive times for %s, "
                    "suppressing drift checks until recovery",
                    self._consecutive_failures,
                    self._lock.lock.entity_id,
                )

        if self._consecutive_failures == POLL_FAILURE_ALERT_THRESHOLD:
            async_create_issue(
                self.hass,
                DOMAIN,
                f"lock_offline_{self._lock.lock.entity_id}",
                is_fixable=False,
                is_persistent=True,
                severity=IssueSeverity.WARNING,
                translation_key="lock_offline",
                translation_placeholders={
                    "lock_entity_id": self._lock.lock.entity_id,
                },
            )

    @property
    def slot_sync_mgrs_suspended(self) -> bool:
        """Return whether slot sync operations are suspended for this lock."""
        return self._slot_sync_mgrs_suspended

    def suspend_slot_sync_mgrs(self) -> None:
        """
        Suspend slot sync managers for this lock.

        Called by any SlotSyncManager that hits a circuit breaker or
        unexpected error. All sync managers for this lock will see the
        flag and stop retrying. Cleared automatically on recovery via
        _reset_backoff (successful poll or push update).
        """
        self._slot_sync_mgrs_suspended = True
        _LOGGER.info("Slot sync suspended for %s", self._lock.lock.entity_id)
        # Notify listeners so other SlotSyncManagers for this lock
        # transition to SUSPENDED on their next _request_sync_check.
        self.async_update_listeners()

    def _reset_backoff(self) -> None:
        """Reset failure counter and restore original update interval."""
        self._slot_sync_mgrs_suspended = False
        if self._consecutive_failures > 0:
            _LOGGER.info(
                "Lock %s recovered after %d consecutive failures",
                self._lock.lock.entity_id,
                self._consecutive_failures,
            )
            self._consecutive_failures = 0
            if self._original_update_interval is not None:
                self.update_interval = self._original_update_interval
        # Unconditionally clear lock_offline issue on any successful poll.
        # Runs outside the if-block so it also clears persisted issues that
        # survive HA restarts (where _consecutive_failures resets to 0).
        async_delete_issue(
            self.hass,
            DOMAIN,
            f"lock_offline_{self._lock.lock.entity_id}",
        )

    async def async_get_usercodes(self) -> dict[int, str | SlotCode]:
        """Fetch usercodes from the provider, normalize slot keys to int, and apply backoff handling."""
        try:
            data = await self._lock.async_internal_get_usercodes()
        except LockCodeManagerError as err:
            self._apply_backoff()
            # During cold start (before the first successful poll), do not
            # raise UpdateFailed. That would fail the initial refresh and
            # keep coordinator-backed entities unavailable until a
            # successful poll completes.
            if not self.last_update_success:
                return {}
            raise UpdateFailed from err

        self._reset_backoff()
        return self._normalize_keys(data)

    async def _async_drift_check(self, now: datetime) -> None:
        """
        Perform periodic drift detection.

        Hard refresh re-fetches codes from the lock to detect out-of-band changes
        (e.g., codes changed at the lock's keypad). If changes are detected,
        updates coordinator data and notifies listeners.
        """
        # Skip if we haven't successfully loaded initial data yet
        if not self.last_update_success:
            return

        if self._consecutive_failures >= BACKOFF_FAILURE_THRESHOLD:
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "Skipping drift check for %s (in backoff after %d failures)",
                self._lock.lock.entity_id,
                self._consecutive_failures,
            )
            return

        _LOGGER.debug(
            "Performing drift detection hard refresh for %s",
            self._lock.lock.entity_id,
        )
        try:
            new_data = self._normalize_keys(
                await self._lock.async_internal_hard_refresh_codes()
            )
        except LockCodeManagerError as err:
            self._apply_backoff()
            _LOGGER.warning(
                "Drift detection hard refresh failed for %s: %s",
                self._lock.lock.entity_id,
                err,
            )
            return

        # Push subscription retry is handled by the config entry state
        # listener and connection transition handler — no need to retry here.

        if new_data != self.data:
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "Drift detected for %s, updating coordinator data",
                self._lock.lock.entity_id,
            )
            self.async_set_updated_data(new_data)

    async def _async_connection_check(self, now: datetime) -> None:
        """Poll connection state so providers can resubscribe on reconnect."""
        try:
            await self._lock.async_internal_is_integration_connected()
        except LockCodeManagerError as err:
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "Connection check failed for %s: %s", self._lock.lock.entity_id, err
            )

    async def async_shutdown(self) -> None:
        """Shut down the coordinator and clean up resources."""
        if self._drift_unsub:
            self._drift_unsub()
            self._drift_unsub = None
        if self._connection_unsub:
            self._connection_unsub()
            self._connection_unsub = None
        await super().async_shutdown()
