18	def _get_instance_id(hass: HomeAssistant) -> str:
19	    """Return the HA instance UUID for PIN masking."""
20	    return hass.data.get(DOMAIN, {}).get("instance_id", "")
21	
22	
23	def _mask_code(
24	    code: str | SlotCode | None, slot_num: int | str, instance_id: str
25	) -> str | None:
26	    """Mask a PIN code for diagnostics output."""
27	    if code is None:
28	        return None
29	    if isinstance(code, SlotCode):
30	        return code.value
31	    if not code:
32	        return "empty"
33	    return mask_pin(code, slot_num, instance_id)
34	
35	
36	_SENSITIVE_UNIQUE_ID_MARKERS = ("|pin", "|code")
37	
38	
39	def _is_sensitive(entry: er.RegistryEntry) -> bool:
40	    """Return True if the entity may expose a PIN or code in its state."""
41	    uid = entry.unique_id or ""
42	    return entry.platform == DOMAIN and any(
43	        m in uid for m in _SENSITIVE_UNIQUE_ID_MARKERS
44	    )
45	
46	
47	def _entity_states_for_device(
48	    hass: HomeAssistant,
49	    ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry,
50	    device_id: str,
51	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
52	    """
53	    Return entity states for all entities on a device.
54	
55	    Sensitive entities (PIN text, code sensor) have their state and
56	    attributes redacted to avoid leaking PINs in diagnostics output.
57	    """
58	    entities: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
59	    for entry in er.async_entries_for_device(ent_reg, device_id):
60	        state = hass.states.get(entry.entity_id)
61	        friendly_name = (
62	            state.attributes.get("friendly_name") if state else entry.original_name
63	        )
64	        if _is_sensitive(entry):
65	            entities.append(
66	                {
67	                    "entity_id": entry.entity_id,
68	                    "friendly_name": friendly_name,
69	                    "platform": entry.platform,
70	                    "state": "**REDACTED**",
71	                    "attributes": {},
72	                }
73	            )
74	        else:
75	            entities.append(
76	                {
77	                    "entity_id": entry.entity_id,
78	                    "friendly_name": friendly_name,
79	                    "platform": entry.platform,
80	                    "state": state.state if state else "unavailable",
81	                    "attributes": dict(state.attributes) if state else {},
82	                }
83	            )
84	    return entities
85	
86	
87	def _lock_diagnostic(
88	    hass: HomeAssistant,
89	    lock: BaseLock,
90	    instance_id: str,
91	    ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry,
92	) -> dict[str, Any]:
93	    """Build diagnostic data for a single lock."""
94	    coordinator = lock.coordinator
95	    coordinator_data = (
96	        {
97	            str(slot): _mask_code(code, slot, instance_id)
98	            for slot, code in coordinator.data.items()
99	        }
100	        if coordinator and coordinator.data
101	        else {}
102	    )
103	
104	    result: dict[str, Any] = {
105	        "entity_id": lock.lock.entity_id,
106	        "domain": lock.domain,
107	        "supports_push": lock.supports_push,
108	        "supports_code_slot_events": lock.supports_code_slot_events,
109	        "coordinator": {
110	            "last_update_success": (
111	                coordinator.last_update_success if coordinator else None
112	            ),
113	            "lock_unreachable": (coordinator.unreachable if coordinator else None),
114	            "data": coordinator_data,
115	        },
116	    }
117	
118	    if lock.device_entry:
119	        result["entities"] = _entity_states_for_device(
120	            hass, ent_reg, lock.device_entry.id
121	        )
122	
123	    return result
124	
125	
126	def _slot_diagnostic(
127	    hass: HomeAssistant,
128	    config_entry: ConfigEntry,
129	    slot_num: int,
130	    locks: dict[str, BaseLock],
131	    instance_id: str,
132	    ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry,
133	    dev_reg: dr.DeviceRegistry,
134	) -> dict[str, Any]:
135	    """Build diagnostic data for a single slot device."""
136	    entry_config = get_entry_config(config_entry)
137	    slot_config = entry_config.slot(slot_num)
138	
139	    raw_pin = slot_config.get("pin")
140	    masked_pin = mask_pin(raw_pin, slot_num, instance_id) if raw_pin else None
141	
142	    result: dict[str, Any] = {
143	        "slot_num": slot_num,
144	        "pin": masked_pin,
145	        "enabled": slot_config.get("enabled"),
146	        "name": slot_config.get("name"),
147	    }
148	
149	    result["locks"] = {
150	        lock_id: (
151	            {
152	                "coordinator_code": _mask_code(
153	                    lock.coordinator.data.get(slot_num), slot_num, instance_id
154	                )
155	            }
156	            if lock.coordinator and lock.coordinator.data
157	            else {}
158	        )
159	        for lock_id, lock in locks.items()
160	        if entry_config.has_lock(lock_id)
161	    }
162	
163	    # Find the slot device and its entities
164	    slot_identifier = (DOMAIN, f"{config_entry.entry_id}|{slot_num}")
165	    device = dev_reg.async_get_device(identifiers={slot_identifier})
166	    if device:
167	        result["entities"] = _entity_states_for_device(hass, ent_reg, device.id)
168	
169	    return result
170	
171	
172	async def async_get_config_entry_diagnostics(
173	    hass: HomeAssistant, config_entry: ConfigEntry
174	) -> dict[str, Any]:
175	    """Return diagnostics for the config entry (superset of all data)."""
176	    instance_id = _get_instance_id(hass)
177	    ent_reg = er.async_get(hass)
178	    dev_reg = dr.async_get(hass)
179	    entry_config = get_entry_config(config_entry)
180	    all_locks: dict[str, BaseLock] = hass.data.get(DOMAIN, {}).get(CONF_LOCKS, {})
181	
182	    return {
183	        "config_entry": {
184	            "entry_id": config_entry.entry_id,
185	            "title": config_entry.title,
186	            "state": config_entry.state.value,
187	        },
188	        "locks": {
189	            lock_id: _lock_diagnostic(hass, lock, instance_id, ent_reg)
190	            for lock_id, lock in all_locks.items()
191	            if entry_config.has_lock(lock_id)
192	        },
193	        "slots": {
194	            str(slot_num): _slot_diagnostic(
195	                hass, config_entry, slot_num, all_locks, instance_id, ent_reg, dev_reg
196	            )
197	            for slot_num in entry_config.slots
198	        },
199	    }
200	
201	
202	async def async_get_device_diagnostics(
203	    hass: HomeAssistant, config_entry: ConfigEntry, device: dr.DeviceEntry
204	) -> dict[str, Any]:
205	    """Return diagnostics for a specific device."""
206	    instance_id = _get_instance_id(hass)
207	    ent_reg = er.async_get(hass)
208	    dev_reg = dr.async_get(hass)
209	    entry_config = get_entry_config(config_entry)
210	    all_locks: dict[str, BaseLock] = hass.data.get(DOMAIN, {}).get(CONF_LOCKS, {})
211	
212	    # Check if this is a slot device: (DOMAIN, entry_id|slot_num)
213	    for identifier in device.identifiers:
214	        if identifier[0] == DOMAIN and "|" in str(identifier[1]):
215	            parts = str(identifier[1]).split("|", 1)
216	            if (
217	                len(parts) == 2
218	                and parts[0] == config_entry.entry_id
219	                and parts[1].isdigit()
220	            ):
221	                slot_num = int(parts[1])
222	                return _slot_diagnostic(
223	                    hass,
224	                    config_entry,

git add custom_components/lock_code_manager/sync.py custom_components/lock_code_manager/coordinator.py custom_components/lock_code_manager/diagnostics.py custom_components/lock_code_manager/websocket.py custom_components/lock_code_manager/resilience.py tests/test_sync.py tests/test_coordinator.py tests/test_binary_sensor.py tests/test_websocket.py tests/test_init.py tests/test_resilience.py && git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
refactor: isolate slot suspension with per-slot CircuitBreaker

Replace the per-slot sync attempt counters with a slot-level CircuitBreaker
and remove the lock-wide suspend flag. A code that won't converge now
suspends only its own lock+slot (per-slot repair issue) instead of freezing
every slot on the lock. Sync managers gate on the coordinator's unreachable
property (the lock breaker) for connectivity suspension, which is still
shared across slots and config entries on the same lock.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -3

content.text: Add `note_connectivity_failure` to the coordinator (near `_apply_backoff`):

input.old_string:
def _apply_backoff(self) -> None:
        """Record a connectivity failure and apply exponential polling backoff."""
input.new_string:
def note_connectivity_failure(self) -> None:
        """
        Record a connectivity failure observed outside the poll path.

        Lets the sync layer feed set/clear transport failures into the same
        lock breaker that polling uses, so "lock is unreachable" converges
        from both code paths.
        """
        self._apply_backoff()

    def _apply_backoff(self) -> None:
        """Record a connectivity failure and apply exponential polling backoff."""

    async def test_set_disconnect_failures_trip_lock_breaker(
        self,
        hass: HomeAssistant,
        mock_lock_config_entry,
        lock_code_manager_config_entry,
    ) -> None:
        """Repeated LockDisconnected on set trips the lock breaker and suspends the tick."""
        manager = get_in_sync_entity_obj(hass, SLOT_1_IN_SYNC_ENTITY)._sync_manager
        manager._coordinator.data[1] = SlotCode.EMPTY

        with patch.object(
            manager,
            "_perform_sync",
            new_callable=AsyncMock,
            side_effect=LockDisconnected("offline"),
        ):
            for _ in range(BACKOFF_FAILURE_THRESHOLD):
                manager._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
                await manager._async_tick()
                await hass.async_block_till_done()

        # Connectivity failures during set converged to "unreachable".
        assert manager._coordinator.unreachable is True

        # The next tick observes the unreachable lock and suspends instead of
        # retrying every tick.
        manager._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
        await manager._async_tick()
        assert manager._state is SyncState.SUSPENDED

input.command:
git add custom_components/lock_code_manager/coordinator.py custom_components/lock_code_manager/sync.py tests/test_sync.py && prek run --files custom_components/lock_code_manager/coordinator.py custom_components/lock_code_manager/sync.py tests/test_sync.py 2>&1 | grep -iE "failed|error" || git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: feed sync transport failures into the lock circuit breaker

A LockDisconnected/LockOperationFailed during a set or clear now records a
failure on the lock breaker via note_connectivity_failure, so an unreachable
lock is detected from the sync path too. Once the breaker trips, sync managers
suspend instead of retrying every tick against a lock that is down.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"; git log --oneline -1
